market_overview.description: Today's LLM market is highly competitive and rapidly evolving, with prices for equivalent performance down dramatically (e.g., GPT-4-level quality now costs a fraction of 2023 prices, with 98% reductions in some cases). The global LLM market segment is part of the broader AI explosion—projected to grow from billions today to $80B+ by 2033 at high CAGRs—driven by enterprise adoption in chatbots, agents, coding, and analysis.

pricing.note: API costs per 1M tokens (USD); input usually cheaper than output by 3-5x; batch discounts common; prices approximate from official sources and may vary slightly by region/volume/commitment. Real costs depend on input:output ratio and optimizations.

openai.name: OpenAI (GPT series)
openai.note: Newer GPT-5.x naming dominates; older GPT-4o references largely superseded.
openai.effective_total_note: Effective total for balanced use often $0.75–$7.50/M for mid-tier equivalents.

anthropic.name: Anthropic (Claude 4.5/4 series)
anthropic.note: Haiku/Sonnet/Opus tiers; batch/caching discounts up to 90% available; 200K context standard.

google.name: Google (Gemini series)
google.note: Strong free tiers + paid; tiered by context size; charges for thinking/grounding tokens in some cases.
google.extras: Free tier (limited RPM/RPD) + caching/grounding extras. Excellent for high-volume or long-context.

xai.name: xAI (Grok series)
xai.note: Competitive with huge 2M context on flagships; fast variants emphasize value.
xai.credits: New users often get free credits ($25+ signup + ongoing via programs).

other.note: Mistral / Cohere / others generally $0.20–$5 input range (mid-tier competitive with Flash/Mini models).
other.open_source: Model download free. Inference via Groq/Together.ai/Fireworks often <$0.10–$0.50/M total (very fast); self-host depends on GPUs (hundreds–thousands/month for high volume).

recommendations.cheapest_for_volume_simple_tasks: Grok Fast ($0.20/$0.50), Gemini Flash-Lite (~$0.10 input), GPT-5 Mini, or Claude Haiku.
recommendations.advice: Test multiple via APIs (many have free credits). Match task to model + optimize aggressively—most production workloads run fine on mid/cheap tiers with 50-90% savings vs. flagship defaults. Market favors users who shop around and tune usage. Prices change frequently; always verify on official sites (openai.com/api/pricing, anthropic.com, ai.google.dev/pricing, docs.x.ai).
